title: CloudFront OAC, Private S3 Origins, and SSE-KMS · Independent Type Trial
summary: An independent TypeScript team receives different tenants, browsers, file sizes, media, regions, compliance, traffic, and cost constraints and transfers cloudfront oac, private s3 origins, and sse-kms into a new private origin authorization contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of private origin authorization contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, configure OAC with always-sign where appropriate and define viewer protocol, origin protocol, request signing, and forwarded Authorization behavior.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, scope the CloudFront service principal to the exact distribution SourceArn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab S3 Origin Website Boundary
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 3649 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate an OAC origin with exact distribution, ownership, and KMS policy; keep AbortSignal and stream cleanup under one named owner; then change object version, signed header, or cache dimension and reject stale authority. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab S3 Origin Website Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, distinguish regular s3 origins from website endpoints by https, oac, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab S3 Origin Website Boundary, keep S3 Origin Website Boundary connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Audit the initial evidence packet · Lab Oai Oac Migration

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate an OAC origin with exact distribution, ownership, and KMS policy; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
